title: Go - Software Engineer, AI
company_focus:
Staffing platform connecting contractors to AI training
projects
description:
Before applying

This role is open to contractors in accepted locations only.
Please confirm your country is on the list before applying —
we're unable to process applications from unlisted
locations. List of accepted countries and locations.

For US applicants

This is a 1099 independent contractor role. It is not
compatible with F-1 OPT, STEM OPT, or any visa status that
requires W-2 employment, guaranteed hours, or employer
sponsorship.

We are unable to provide offer letters or employment
verification for this role.

What You'll Be Doing

Help train large language models (LLMs) to write production-
grade code across a wide range of programming languages:

- Compare and rank multiple code snippets, explaining which is
  best and why
- Repair and refactor AI-generated code for correctness,
  efficiency, and style
- Inject feedback (ratings, edits, test results) into the RLHF
  pipeline and keep it running smoothly

End result: the model learns to propose, critique, and
improve code the way you do.

RLHF in one line: Generate code → expert engineers rank,
edit, and justify → convert that feedback into reward
signals → reinforcement learning tunes the model toward code
you'd actually ship.

What You'll Need

- 3+ years of professional software engineering experience in
  Go (constraint programming experience is a bonus, but not
  required)
- Strong code-review instincts — you can spot logic errors,
  performance traps, and security issues quickly
- Extreme attention to detail and excellent written
  communication skills. Much of this role involves
  explaining why one approach is better than another. This
  cannot be overstated.
- Comfortable reading documentation and language specs, and
  able to work well in an asynchronous, low-oversight
  environment

Identity verification: Applicants will be required to verify
their identity and confirm they have valid documentation to
work as an independent contractor in their country of
residence.

What You Don't Need

- No prior RLHF or AI training experience

Logistics

- Location: Fully remote — work from anywhere on the accepted
  locations list
- Compensation: $30–$70/hr based on location and seniority.
  Note: the majority of projects run at around $30/hr —
  higher rates apply to senior profiles and specific project
  types
- Hours: Minimum 15 hrs/week, up to 40+ hrs/week available —
  hours vary by project and are not guaranteed week to week
- Engagement: 1099 independent contractor
- Payment: Weekly via PayPal or Stripe

⚠️ Important: Hours are project-dependent and can vary week
to week. We recommend keeping other work options open
alongside this engagement rather than relying on it as your
sole source of income.
responsibilities: Compare and rank multiple code snippets generated by LLMs, explaining which is best and why
Repair and refactor AI-generated code for correctness, efficiency, and style
Inject feedback including ratings, edits, and test results into the RLHF pipeline
Provide detailed written justifications for code quality assessments to train model behavior
Review code for logic errors, performance traps, and security issues
Work asynchronously in a low-oversight remote environment
use_cases: Training LLMs to write production-grade code across multiple programming languages
RLHF-based code model improvement through expert human feedback
AI code generation quality improvement via ranking and editing
Reinforcement learning reward signal generation from expert code reviews
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role directly involves training LLMs through RLHF — the
engineer compares, ranks, repairs, and refactors AI-
generated code, injecting feedback into the RLHF pipeline to
improve model outputs. While the work is human feedback
rather than building models programmatically, it is hands-on
work shaping model behavior, which qualifies as ai-first.
